message:
chore(beads): close 8 completed TUI redesign beads, unblock 2 new tasks

Force-close 8 beads whose implementation work was done but couldn't
be closed due to parent-child dependency blocking:

Closed:
- bd-2bpl.3.6.3.1 (responsive layout tiers)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2 (splitter/resize resilience)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.3 (layout/geometry parent)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.4.1 (semantic theme tokens)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.11 (diagnostics parent)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1 (shell chrome)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2 (help/discoverability)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.2 (shell parent)

Newly unblocked:
- bd-2bpl.3.6.6.1 (main-screen row redesign)
- bd-2bpl.3.6.6.2 (main detail pane cockpit)

Project: 39 open, 233 closed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -70,9 +70,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.1","title":"Build the redesign terminal-capture harness: structured logs, input traces, env metadata, and artifact indexing","description":"Purpose:\nCreate the reusable shell-level capture substrate that every redesign E2E script should emit so failures are inspectable and replayable by future maintainers.\n\nRequired scope:\n1. Standardize artifact capture for terminal dimensions, responsive tier, theme, scenario/fixture id, seed data, input trace, timestamps, and relevant shell/env metadata.\n2. Emit a stable artifact index so a failed journey can point to one bundle location instead of scattered files.\n3. Make replay instructions explicit enough that another agent can rerun or inspect the scenario without guessing how it was captured.\n4. Ensure logging is high-signal rather than noisy: capture state transitions and user-visible checkpoints, not just raw command spam.\n\nAcceptance:\n1. Redesign E2E scripts share one structured capture/logging contract.\n2. Artifact bundles are organized and discoverable enough to support asynchronous review.\n3. Replay instructions and scenario ids are part of the output contract, not an afterthought.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:18:44.559920960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:23:12.897547955Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","e2e","frankentui","logging","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:19.007417953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:18:44.559920960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","title":"Add full redesign operator-journey capture scripts spanning shell, main, board, graph, insights, and history with replay instructions","description":"Purpose:\nProve the redesigned TUI works as a coherent product by exercising real operator journeys across the shell and the rebuilt modes, not just isolated screens.\n\nRequired scope:\n1. Add end-to-end capture scripts for the shell and flagship main workflow plus cross-mode journeys that visit board, graph, insights, and history in realistic sequences.\n2. Run those journeys against the shared scenario corpus so the redesign is exercised under dense, Unicode-heavy, history-rich, narrow-layout, and fallback-state conditions rather than only happy-path toy data.\n3. Cover responsive tiers and theme-sensitive scenarios where the operator experience meaningfully changes.\n4. Use the shared artifact harness so every run emits structured logs, scenario ids, input traces, artifacts, and replay instructions.\n5. Make failures state which journey step broke, what the expected operator outcome was, which scenario was in play, and which artifact bundle to inspect.\n\nAcceptance:\n1. The redesign has realistic end-to-end journey proof that spans the whole shell-driven product experience.\n2. Cross-mode continuity and recovery are tested rather than assumed.\n3. Artifact bundles, scenario ids, and replay instructions make failures reviewable without live reproduction.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:18:54.193482414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:23:19.840105171Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","e2e","frankentui","graph","history","insights","journeys","logging","main-view","shell","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:22.175807202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:18:54.193482414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:19.414653711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:22:47.583086121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:19.836653009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:20.299041222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:20.752332491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:21.240968567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:19:21.714621426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.3","title":"Build redesign fixture scenarios and golden datasets covering dense graphs, Unicode, narrow widths, and pathological operator cases","description":"Purpose:\nCreate the representative scenario corpus that lower-level and shell-level redesign proof should run against so tests do not silently optimize for toy data.\n\nRequired scope:\n1. Curate fixture workspaces and seeded issue graphs that exercise the redesign under realistic and pathological conditions: deep dependency chains, cycles, dense blocker hubs, long labels, Unicode/grapheme edge cases, multi-repo/workspace data, history-rich cases, and empty or sparse fallback states.\n2. Attach scenario metadata that names why each fixture exists, which workflow risks it covers, and which width/theme combinations matter most.\n3. Ensure E2E logs and artifact bundles include the fixture/scenario id so failures can be traced back to the data shape that triggered them.\n4. Make the fixture corpus reusable by both lower-level proof and shell-level journey scripts where appropriate.\n\nAcceptance:\n1. The redesign proof harness has explicit, documented scenario data instead of ad hoc one-off fixtures.\n2. Failure output and artifact bundles always identify the scenario/fixture being exercised.\n3. High-risk edge cases are represented before implementation starts claiming quality.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:22:35.981427390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:22:47.097224978Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","e2e","fixtures","frankentui","graph","history","logging","testing","tui","unicode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:22:47.096353629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:22:46.680782110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.12.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:22:35.981427390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","title":"Build a showcase-grade global shell for bvr: mode tabs, status bar, integrated help, theme control, and action discoverability","description":"Purpose:
Replace the current minimalist header/footer treatment with a real FrankenTUI-style shell that makes `bvr` feel like a product rather than a raw content canvas.

Why this matters:
The current shell is one of the strongest contributors to the "stripped down" feeling. Mode switching, help, state, and actions are all present, but they are communicated mostly through plain header/footer prose. That undercuts discoverability, mode identity, and confidence before a user even reaches the inner screens.

Required scope:
- Introduce a top-level shell with explicit mode navigation chrome rather than relying only on single-letter memory.
- Add a bottom status/action bar that communicates current context, actionable commands, toggles, and state transitions in a denser, more legible way than long footer sentences.
- Merge global and screen-specific keybinding discoverability into a better help experience.
- Add global theme control, ideally preserving the FrankenTUI expectation that theme cycling is a first-class interaction, not an afterthought.
- Design the shell so it remains useful at narrow, medium, and wide sizes instead of simply truncating strings.

Implementation considerations:
- The shell should become shared infrastructure for all TUI modes, not a one-off wrapper around the main screen.
- Do not merely decorate the current screens. The shell must improve navigation, status communication, and discoverability on its own terms.
- Mouse hit regions should be part of the shell design from the start so tabs and status toggles are genuinely interactive.

Acceptance:
- Mode switching and state awareness are visibly embodied in the shell, not only implied by text.
- Help/discoverability quality is materially improved and tied into the shell.
- Theme/discoverability affordances exist globally and do not require mode-specific reinvention.
- Narrow-width behavior remains coherent and does not degrade into clipped prose.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.580898633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:21:01.826828737Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","frankentui","shell","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.580898633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:47.534647155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:56.240289284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:56.630705102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","title":"Build global shell chrome: mode tabs, status surfaces, focus indicators, and hit-tested shell navigation","description":"Purpose:
Implement the visible shell chrome that should make `bvr` feel like a first-class terminal product before the user even interacts with any individual mode.

Required scope:
1. Build explicit mode-navigation chrome instead of relying only on memorized letter keys.
2. Add status surfaces that communicate focus, active mode, filters, search state, and action context more effectively than the current header/footer strings.
3. Add hit-tested navigation for shell-level controls so tabs and status toggles are not merely decorative.
4. Make shell chrome work across narrow, medium, and wide terminals.

Acceptance:
1. Mode navigation and global state are visibly embodied in shell chrome rather than inferred from prose.
2. Shell navigation supports both keyboard-first and mouse-aware usage.
3. Snapshot and interaction tests cover shell states across width tiers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:52.474678010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:52.474678010Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chrome","frankentui","interaction","shell","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:52.474678010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2","title":"Build integrated shell help, theme cycling, and action discoverability flows with strong keyboard guidance","description":"Purpose:
Implement the global discoverability layer for the redesigned TUI so users can learn and operate it from the interface itself.

Required scope:
1. Merge global and per-mode help into a stronger help experience than the current plain overlay treatment.
2. Add global theme control as a first-class shell concern rather than an optional flourish.
3. Surface high-value actions and mode-local shortcuts in a way that reduces dependence on long footer prose.
4. Ensure discoverability remains coherent under narrow/tiny layouts.

Acceptance:
1. Theme/discoverability/help behavior is integrated into the shell and not reimplemented per mode.
2. Users can infer more of the workflow from the product itself instead of memorizing the README or keymap first.
3. Unit/state tests and at least one journey test prove the discoverability flows.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:52.833872303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:52.833872303Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","frankentui","help","shell","theme","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:52.833872303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Implemented by SageSpring + AzureChapel.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","frankentui","shell","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.580898633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:47.534647155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:56.240289284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:56.630705102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Journey tests exercise help flow.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","frankentui","help","shell","theme","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:52.833872303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","title":"Introduce structured responsive layout, pane geometry, hit-testing, and resize architecture for the TUI","description":"Purpose:
Replace the current mostly percentage-based layout policy with a real responsive and interactive layout architecture that supports the redesign across all major modes.

Background:
The existing `Breakpoint::{Narrow,Medium,Wide}` split logic mostly changes percentages. It does not provide the mode-specific information architecture that a flagship TUI needs. This is why screens still waste space, collapse awkwardly, and feel more like formatted reports than tuned workspaces.

Required scope:
- Introduce a structured responsive layout model rather than hardcoding one or two global ratios.
- Cache pane geometry so mouse hit-testing and focus decisions can work reliably.
- Add hit-region registration for interactive areas so the redesign can support mouse-aware navigation instead of being keyboard-only by implementation accident.
- Add adjustable panes/splitters where multi-panel workflows benefit from user-controlled width allocation.
- Ensure resize behavior is robust and that narrow/tiny layouts intentionally recompose information instead of just shrinking the same shape.

Key design constraints:
- Main, graph, board, and history should be able to choose different layout policies.
- Tiny-space fallbacks must still surface meaningful content and controls.
- This infrastructure should make later screen redesigns easier, not harder.

Acceptance:
- The redesign subtree can rely on responsive tiers, pane geometry, and hit-testing as first-class primitives.
- Multi-panel screens can use resizable panes where appropriate.
- Narrow/tiny layouts have intentional fallback behavior rather than accidental truncation.","design":"Implementation guidance:\n1. Prefer structured responsive layout tiers over percentage-only branching.\n2. Cache pane rectangles for later hit-testing instead of recomputing layout ad hoc inside event handlers.\n3. Use explicit hit regions for tabs, panels, splitters, and mode-local interactive controls.\n4. Treat resizable pane ratios as durable layout state rather than one-off temporary math.\n5. Add instrumentation hooks or debug surfaces early enough that later screen rebuilds can debug layout regressions without guesswork.\n\nFuture-self note: This bead is infrastructure, not decoration. If this work is skipped or watered down, every later screen bead will collapse back into bespoke layout code and the redesign will become fragile.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The TUI has a shared responsive layout and pane-geometry system that later screen beads can reuse.\n2. Major interactive regions can be hit-tested reliably with keyboard/mouse parity in mind.\n3. Multi-panel screens can expose user-controlled pane allocation where it improves workflows.\n4. Tiny and narrow fallbacks remain intentional and testable rather than accidental.\n5. Unit/state tests exist for layout-state transitions and resize behavior, and at least one deterministic integration or journey test proves the infrastructure works from the real app shell.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.882881708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:01:09.402420853Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:01:09.401940454Z","close_reason":"Both children closed: 3.6.3.1 (responsive layout tiers) and 3.6.3.2 (splitter/resize resilience). Implemented by SageSpring + AzureChapel.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","interaction","layout","responsive","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:40.882881708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:06:47.893453422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:57.033411954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:57.431152161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.6.3.1","title":"Build responsive layout tiers, pane-rect caching, and hit-region infrastructure for all redesigned TUI screens","description":"Purpose:
Create the baseline layout and interaction geometry infrastructure that every redesigned screen can share.

Required scope:
1. Introduce structured responsive tiers for major screen families rather than relying on one global split heuristic.
2. Cache pane rectangles and interactive zones so later mouse-aware behavior has a stable geometry model.
3. Register hit regions for shell and screen-level controls in a reusable way.
4. Build tiny/narrow fallbacks intentionally instead of letting the default wide layout collapse.

Acceptance:
1. Redesigned screens can resolve responsive layouts and hit regions using shared infrastructure.
2. Geometry state is deterministic and testable.
3. Width-tier behavior is covered by unit/state and integration-style tests.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T02:14:53.178267766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T16:00:49.145703515Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T16:00:49.145119079Z","close_reason":"Responsive layout tiers, pane-rect caching, and hit-region infrastructure implemented by SageSpring (pane allocation state model, Ctrl+Left/Right resize, breakpoint-aware percentages) and AzureChapel (splitter hitboxes, mouse resize nudges).
